text.title: Why design + impl-plan were merged
text.summary: The merge is empirical, not aesthetic. F021, F022, F002 each showed the two files cross-referencing on every section and drifting whenever one was updated alone.

section.title: Observed failure pattern
section.p: In F021 a schema decision in design.md changed late; implementation-plan.md still referenced the old shape; one task generated code against stale design. The fix landed in a second PR and burned a half-day reconciling. F022 and F002 reproduced the same pattern at different scales.

rule.statement: Two-file design + impl-plan is forbidden going forward. Single plan.md only.
rule.rationale: Atomic update beats two-file sync every time. The merge removes a recurring failure point.

list.item: The DESIGN content — still required, just inside the same file.
The EXECUTION PLAN content — still required, same file.
Cross-references between them — now markdown anchors within plan.md, not cross-file links.
Separate file headers, separate frontmatter, separate version numbers.
The cross-file linking discipline that nobody actually followed.
